name: Baidu Pan
summary: Baidu's large-scale cloud storage service with multi-device backup, file sharing, iPhone/Mac apps, an official PCS open platform, and very large paid expansion tiers.

max_file_size.notes: Baidu's official help center says ordinary users can upload up to 1 GB per file on the web and up to 4 GB per file in the Windows and Mac desktop clients. Paid members can upload up to 10 GB per file in the desktop clients, and super members up to 20 GB.

max_file_size_account.notes: Baidu's official help center says ordinary account holders can upload up to 1 GB per file on the web and up to 4 GB per file in the Windows and Mac desktop clients. Paid members can upload up to 10 GB per file in the desktop clients, and super members up to 20 GB.

retention.notes: Files remain until deleted. Baidu's official help center says deleted files stay in the recycle bin for 10 days on ordinary accounts, 15 days for members, and 30 days for super members before permanent deletion.

storage.notes: The cited first-party pages still do not cleanly expose one stable default free-storage quota in accessible text. Current official help says every account has a free quota, mobile-app login can grant surprise bonus capacity, and some promotional capacity can expire, but it still does not publish one reliable base free number we can promote into the dataset.

bandwidth.notes: Bandwidth is conditional. Baidu's current official help says the Mac client does not impose upload or download speed throttling, but the cited first-party pages still do not publish one clean universal free-user traffic quota across web, desktop, and sharing flows.

account.benefits: A Baidu account is required for cloud backup, syncing, link sharing, and API-authorized access across devices, but current user-provided tests indicate the registration and SMS-login flows are not working reliably for Bangladeshi phone numbers.

developer.notes: Baidu publishes an official PCS open platform for personal cloud storage with file APIs, structured-data APIs, OAuth-based access, and SDK downloads, but the cited official pages still do not present a simple end-user CLI workflow.

security.notes: The App Store listing says the service uses multiple data centers and cites ISO27001 and ISO27018 security certifications. The official help center also says users can protect shared links with passwords and set automatic link expiry, but it still does not claim end-to-end encryption.

name: Upload.ee
summary: Estonian file host with anonymous uploads, broad file-type support, and clear free retention rules for guest and registered users.

max_file_size.notes: The FAQ currently says unregistered users can upload up to 100 MB files, while registered users can upload up to 200 MB.

retention.notes: The FAQ and rules currently say anonymous files expire 50 days after last download, while registered users' files expire after 120 days.

storage.notes: The cited public pages do not publish a total storage quota for the service.

max_file_size_account.notes: The FAQ currently says registered users can upload files up to 200 MB.

account.benefits: Anonymous uploads are supported; registered users get larger uploads, longer retention, galleries, and privacy controls.

developer.notes: The cited public pages show iframe embedding and an old Java upload helper, but they do not document a public developer API or CLI workflow.

security.notes: The cited public pages do not claim end-to-end encryption.

summary: No-account file hosting service with password-protected links, remote URL uploads, and a clearly published max upload size on the public upload form.

retention.notes: The current pricing page says free uploads are deleted 14 days after the last download; registered free accounts are kept 17 days after the last download.

storage.notes: The cited public pages do not publish a free storage quota.

bandwidth.notes: The current pricing page lists 54 GB/day for free uploads and 73 GB/day for registered free accounts.

max_file_size_guest.notes: The public upload form and current pricing page list a 5250 MB maximum size for free guest uploads.

name: vgy.me
summary: Simple image host with anonymous uploads, a public upload API, and longer retention for account-linked images.

max_file_size.notes: The homepage says all users get 20 MB per image.

retention.notes: Retention is conditional. Anonymous uploads are deleted after 6 months without views, while account-linked uploads are deleted after 1 year without views.

storage.notes: Storage is conditional. vgy.me says there is no restriction on how much disk space you can use, but mass uploads that consume too much space without approval may be deleted and banned.

bandwidth.notes: Unlimited. The homepage says there is no speed throttling.

account.benefits: Anonymous uploads work without an account. Free accounts let users link uploads to an account and generate a user key for integrations.
